data.title: MARA Holdings acquires 64% of French HPC company Exaion, partnering with EDF and NJJ to grow European data centers.
data.description: MARA, a prominent Bitcoin miner, has successfully acquired a majority stake in Exaion. This strategic move involves partnering with EDF, a well-known energy company, and Xavier Niel's NJJ. The collaboration aims to enhance and expand European data center operations. This acquisition marks a significant development in the cryptocurrency sector, signifying MARA's commitment to growth and innovation.

data.title: Inventor from Summerville, S.C. develops new cat litter box called PRO-VENT to reduce odors/messes.
data.description: A resident of Summerville, South Carolina, mentioned that they were motivated to create a redesigned litter box to address odor and mess issues. This led to the invention of the PRO-VENT. The inventor stated that their creation provides a distinctive and improved space for cats to handle their basic needs. This invention was developed in 2026 in Pittsburgh.

data.title: S&P Global executives to present at Raymond James' 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference on March 3, 2026. CEO and CFO to participate.
data.description: On March 3, 2026, Martina Cheung, President and CEO, and Eric Aboaf, CFO, of S&P Global (NYSE: SPGI), will attend Raymond James' 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference. The session will be webcast for wider access and insight into the company's developments.

data.title: Bitcoin price unaffected by Supreme Court ruling on illegal tariffs, discussions of $150B refund.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin remained stable within a "descending trend" as the Supreme Court ruled that certain US trade tariffs were unlawful and subject to reimbursement.

data.title: Axelspace与Tri-Sat Constellation Co.签约合同，推进日本防卫省卫星星座项目，旨在实现“让太空变得触手可及”。
data.description: Tokyo - Leading microsatellite developer and operator, Axelspace Corporation ("Axelspace"), dedicated to making space more accessible, has announced a contract signing for optical image data acquisition with Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd. and Mitsui Bussan Aerospace Co., Ltd., as part of advancing Japan's Ministry of Defense satellite constellation project. Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd. is a Special Purpose Company (SPC) jointly established by Mitsubishi Electric Corporation, SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation, and Mitsui & Co., Ltd. The Ministry of Defense satellite constellation project involves Mitsubishi Electric Corporation, SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation, Mitsui & Co., Ltd., Synspective Inc., Institute for Q-shu Pioneers of Space, Inc., Mitsui Bussan Aerospace Co., Ltd., among others.

data.title: Deere & Co beat Q1 earnings expectations with $2.42 EPS vs $2.06 estimate. Revenue rose to $9.611 billion from $7.686 billion. CEO optimistic on recovery. Forecasted net income between $4.5 billion to $5.0 billion for fiscal 2026.
data.description: Deere & Company (NYSE: DE) exceeded expectations in its first quarter, with EPS at $2.42 surpassing the estimated $2.06. Global net sales and revenues rose by 13% to $9.611 billion, well above the $7.686 billion estimate. Chairman and CEO John May expressed optimism amidst challenges in the large agriculture sector, noting a rebound in demand for construction and small agriculture. Deere increased its fiscal 2026 net income guidance to between $4.5 billion and $5.0 billion. The company anticipates a 5-10% decline in Production & Precision Agriculture net sales, a 15% rise in Small Agriculture & Turf net sales, and a 15% increase in Construction & Forestry net sales. Financial Services net income is expected to... (Continued on Benzinga.com)

data.title: Proposed settlement and dismissal with prejudice of shareholder derivative litigation of Tout Inc. in Jacksonville.
data.description: Jacksonville, Florida - On February 20, 2026, a notice was released to all owners of Tout Inc. shares regarding a proposed settlement and dismissal with prejudice of shareholder derivative litigation involving the company. Owners are urged to review the notice entirely.

data.title: Praxis Precision Medicine reported Q4 losses of $3.50/share, wider than expected. Analysts raised forecasts. CEO optimistic about company's future.
data.description: Praxis Precision Medicines, Inc. revealed a larger-than-anticipated loss in Q4 with quarterly losses amounting to $3.50 per share, falling short of the projected $3.08 per share loss. The company's CEO, Marcio Souza, highlighted significant progress made in clinical and regulatory developments throughout their portfolio in the fourth quarter of 2025. They commenced 2026 with the submission of two NDAs for ulixacaltamide and relutrigine, anticipating positive reviews to transition to a commercial entity. Additionally, the company's vormatrigine and elsunersen programs are set to disclose topline results in early 2026, aligning with their schedule for further NDA activities.

data.title: BTC treasury executives advocate for changing a 1,250% risk weight in Basel III, while private equity at 400% is second highest.
data.description: Under the current Basel III banking framework, private equity, considered to have the second-highest risk weighting, bears a weight of 400%. This means that banks must allocate capital amounting to four times the initial investment in private equity to cover potential risks.

data.title: Yeti's Q4 earnings were 92 cents per share, beating estimates of 88 cents. Company's sales hit $583.708 million. FY26 EPS guidance slightly below expectations. Sales estimated at $1.980-$2.017 billion. CEO highlighted a strong Q4 performance.
data.description: YETI Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: YETI) released its fourth-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. The company reported quarterly earnings of 92 cents per share, exceeding the anticipated 88 cents per share. Quarterly sales stood at $583.708 million, also exceeding the estimated $582.455 million. Looking ahead, YETI anticipates an adjusted EPS for FY2026 of $2.77-$2.83, falling slightly below market estimates of $2.83. Sales for the fiscal year are projected to range between $1.980 billion-$2.017 billion, slightly above the estimated $1.975 billion. Matt Reintjes, President and CEO, noted that Q4 was the company's most robust quarter. For more details, refer to the original article.

data.title: Axelspace與Tri-Sat Constellation及Mitsui Bussan Aerospace合作推進日本防衛省衛星星座專案。
data.description: Tokyo - Axelspace Corporation ("Axelspace"), a leading developer and operator of microsatellites dedicated to making space more accessible, has announced a partnership with Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd. and Mitsui Bussan Aerospace Co., Ltd. to advance the satellite constellation project for the Japanese Ministry of Defense. Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd. is a special purpose company jointly established by Mitsubishi Electric Corporation, SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation, and Mitsui & Co., Ltd. The project also involves Mitsubishi Electric Corporation, SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation, Mitsui & Co., Ltd., Synspective Inc., Institute for Q-shu Pioneers of Space, Inc., and Mitsui Bussan Aerospace Co., Ltd. The collaboration includes an optical imaging data acquisition contract to support the project.

data.title: Tandem Diabetes Care (NASDAQ: TNDM) reports Q4 earnings of 1 cent per share with sales at $290.400 million, surpassing estimates. FY2026 sales expected at $1.065-1.085 billion. CEO expresses positive outlook.
data.description: In its recent report, Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. (NASDAQ: TNDM) exceeded expectations with its fourth-quarter financial performance. The company recorded a quarterly loss of 1 cent per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of 8 cents per share in losses. Additionally, Tandem Diabetes Care achieved quarterly sales of $290.4 million, outperforming the analyst consensus of $277.029 million in sales. Looking forward, Tandem Diabetes Care anticipates FY2026 sales between $1.065 billion and $1.085 billion, contrasting with market estimates of $1.103 billion. President and CEO John Sheridan expressed satisfaction with the achievements in 2025, highlighting surpassing $1 billion in global sales, setting gross margin records, and enhancing commercial operations and business models. The company aims to continue this success in 2026 by strengthening its operations.
